message:
fix(ui): replace × text with IconX SVG for consistent overlay close buttons

- Add IconX component to icons.tsx (Feather-style X icon)
- Update Overlay.tsx to use IconX instead of × text character
- Update mobile hint text from '×' to 'X' for consistency
- Remove fontSize property (now controlled by IconX size prop)

This ensures:
- Consistent rendering across all browsers and fonts
- Better accessibility (aria-label already exists)
- Visual consistency with our icon system's stroke weight
- Clean SVG scaling at all resolutions

Closes: phage_explorer-anf8

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"phage_explorer-anf8","title":"UX: Replace × character with IconX SVG in overlay close buttons","description":"## Problem

The Overlay component uses a text character × (multiplication sign or letter x) for close buttons instead of our IconX SVG component. This causes:

1. **Inconsistent rendering** - Different fonts render × differently across browsers
2. **Accessibility concerns** - Screen readers may announce it as 'multiplication' or 'times'
3. **Visual inconsistency** - Doesn't match our icon system's stroke weight/style
4. **Scaling issues** - Text characters don't scale as cleanly as SVGs

Confirmed via Playwright screenshots of Settings overlay and other modals.

## Solution

Replace the × character with IconX SVG component:

```tsx
import { IconX } from '../ui';

<button onClick={handleClose} aria-label='Close overlay'>
  <IconX size={20} />
</button>
```

## Files

- packages/web/src/components/overlays/Overlay.tsx
- packages/web/src/components/ui/icons.tsx (verify IconX exists)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Close button uses IconX SVG
- Renders consistently across Chrome, Firefox, Safari
- aria-label preserved","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:48:37.456798152-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T16:48:37.456798152-06:00"}
+{"id":"phage_explorer-anf8","title":"UX: Replace × character with IconX SVG in overlay close buttons","description":"## Problem

The Overlay component uses a text character × (multiplication sign or letter x) for close buttons instead of our IconX SVG component. This causes:

1. **Inconsistent rendering** - Different fonts render × differently across browsers
2. **Accessibility concerns** - Screen readers may announce it as 'multiplication' or 'times'
3. **Visual inconsistency** - Doesn't match our icon system's stroke weight/style
4. **Scaling issues** - Text characters don't scale as cleanly as SVGs

Confirmed via Playwright screenshots of Settings overlay and other modals.

## Solution

Replace the × character with IconX SVG component:

```tsx
import { IconX } from '../ui';

<button onClick={handleClose} aria-label='Close overlay'>
  <IconX size={20} />
</button>
```

## Files

- packages/web/src/components/overlays/Overlay.tsx
- packages/web/src/components/ui/icons.tsx (verify IconX exists)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Close button uses IconX SVG
- Renders consistently across Chrome, Firefox, Safari
- aria-label preserved","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T16:48:37.456798152-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T17:15:51.657447801-06:00"}
 {"id":"phage_explorer-ao55","title":"Pillar 3: Foundational Knowledge Modules","description":"## Core Educational Lessons

### What This Creates
Five standalone educational modules that can be accessed from a 'Learn' menu or linked from context help.

### Module 1: What Is a Bacteriophage?
**Content:**
- Phages as bacterial viruses (most abundant biological entities on Earth)
- Basic structure: head (capsid), tail, tail fibers
- Size context (nanometers, smaller than bacteria)
- Historical significance (Hershey-Chase, genetic code discovery)
- Modern relevance (phage therapy, biotechnology tools)

**Visuals:**
- Annotated phage diagram (T4-like, filamentous, tailless)
- Size comparison (phage vs bacteria vs human cell)
- Electron microscopy gallery

### Module 2: DNA Basics for Engineers
**Content:**
- DNA as information storage (compare to binary/text encoding)
- Four bases (A, T, G, C) as alphabet
- Double helix structure and base pairing rules
- Directionality (5' to 3') - like reading direction
- Complementary strands

**Visuals:**
- Animated base pairing (A-T, G-C)
- Interactive double helix (rotate, zoom)
- ASCII art comparison (ATGC vs 01)

### Module 3: The Central Dogma
**Content:**
- DNA → RNA → Protein pipeline
- Transcription (DNA to mRNA)
- Translation (mRNA to protein)
- Why this matters (sequence predicts function)
- Phage context (minimal genomes, overlapping genes)

**Visuals:**
- Animated flow diagram
- Ribosome 'reading' mRNA
- Before/after: sequence vs protein

### Module 4: The Genetic Code
**Content:**
- Codons as 3-letter words (64 combinations, 20 amino acids)
- Degeneracy (multiple codons → same amino acid)
- Start/stop codons
- Reading frames (shift by 1 → completely different protein)
- Why codon bias matters

**Visuals:**
- Interactive codon wheel/table
- Reading frame shifter demo
- Amino acid property wheel

### Module 5: Phage Lifecycle
**Content:**
- Lytic cycle (attach → inject → replicate → lyse)
- Lysogenic cycle (integrate → dormant → trigger)
- Temperate vs virulent phages
- Why this affects analysis (prophages in bacterial genomes)

**Visuals:**
- Animated infection cycle
- Lambda decision circuit simplified
- Prophage integration diagram

### Technical Implementation
- Each module as separate component with internal navigation
- Progress tracking (which modules completed)
- Cross-links to glossary terms
- 'Try it' sections linking to actual app features

### Files to Create
- packages/web/src/education/modules/WhatIsPhage.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/modules/DNABasics.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/modules/CentralDogma.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/modules/GeneticCode.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/modules/PhageLifecycle.tsx
- packages/web/src/education/modules/ModuleViewer.tsx (container)
- packages/web/src/education/modules/index.ts

### Why These Five
These modules cover the minimum knowledge needed to understand what Phage Explorer shows. They build on each other: phages → DNA → central dogma → genetic code → lifecycle.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:32:54.277351-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:32:54.277351-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-ao55","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-ty1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:32:54.279422-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
